LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.in: Check the current item rotation progress and enter this edition's collectible exchange shop.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.pr: Current item rotation progress: all first-edition goods have been unlocked.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.we: Welcome to Migua's Little Shop. This edition is small, but everything was picked with care.
You came at just the right time. The first-edition shelves are ready, so take your time.
Come in already. Do not stand in the doorway. If you want to exchange for something, check the right side.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.br: Bring this home and it can be useful right away.
The stock on that one is limited. If you really need it, do not wait too long.
I did not just make up these prices.
Everything on the right is part of this edition's list. Next edition may be different.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.id: Take your time. The shelves have not been emptied yet.
Some things look ordinary until you suddenly need them.
If you are just passing by, you can still see how much stock this edition has left.
I do rotate the goods regularly, but for this edition, this is the lineup.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.pu: Deal. The goods will be sent to you. Watch for the drop pod.
All right, it is on the books. One more batch sold.
Received. The goods will be delivered soon.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.no: No good. You do not have enough money.
You are a little short. Go check the little treasury and come back.
If you do not have enough money, do not force the exchange. The goods are not going to grow legs and run away.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopOne.it: Simple meals suited for temporary supplies.
Industrial medicine prepared for small wounds and sudden infections. Stock is limited.
Common industrial parts that can fill gaps in equipment repair and quick construction.
Not exactly essential, but good for leaving a little sweetness for someone after a long day.
A small batch of steel exchanged for gold, used to test per-item currency configuration.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.in: Check the current item rotation progress and enter this edition's collectible exchange shop.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.pr: Current item rotation progress: all second-edition goods have been unlocked.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.we: Welcome to Migua's Little Shop. This edition is small, but everything was picked with care.
You came at just the right time. The second-edition shelves are ready, so take your time.
Come in already. Do not stand in the doorway. If you want to exchange for something, check the right side.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.br: Bring this home and it can be useful right away.
The stock on that one is limited. If you really need it, do not wait too long.
I did not just make up these prices.
Everything on the right is part of this edition's list. Next edition may be different.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.id: Take your time. The shelves have not been emptied yet.
Some things look ordinary until you suddenly need them.
If you are just passing by, you can still see how much stock this edition has left.
I do rotate the goods regularly, but for this edition, this is the lineup.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.pu: Deal. The goods will be sent to you. Watch for the drop pod.
All right, it is on the books. One more batch sold.
Received. The goods will be delivered soon.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.no: No good. You do not have enough money.
You are a little short. Go check the little treasury and come back.
If you do not have enough money, do not force the exchange. The goods are not going to grow legs and run away.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopTwo.it: Simple meals suited for temporary supplies.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_MiguaShopCollec: Migua's Little Shop
Migua's Little Shop
Collectibles
Check the current item rotation progress and enter this edition's collectible exchange shop.
Collectibles
Current item rotation progress: all collectibles have been unlocked.
Welcome to Migua's Little Shop. This time, everything here is a rare treasure!
You came at just the right time. Want to add something shiny to the colony?
This time it is... super collectibles!
Why are you looking at me? I am not for sale!
Bring this home and it will be useful right away! ...I mean, it really is worth it!
The stock on that one is limited. These are priceless treasures, so if you really need it, do not wait too long.
I did not just make up these prices. If you think they are expensive, I think they are expensive too.
Everything on the right is good stuff. Next edition... probably there will not be a next edition!
Take your time. Customers with enough money for these things are almost impossible to find on the rim.
Some things do not look ordinary, and when you really need them... wait, collectibles probably do not have that problem.
If you are just passing by to look, that is fine too.
I do rotate goods regularly, but for this edition, this is the lineup. Honestly, it is hard to imagine another edition replacing these collectibles!
Deal. The goods will be sent to you. Watch for the drop pod.
All right, it is on the books!
Thanks for the purchase!
Received. The goods will be delivered soon.
Your silver reserves are insufficient.
You are a little short. Go check the little treasury and come back.
If you do not have enough money, do not force the exchange. Collectibles are not going to grow legs and run away.
Stanford Piano
A very, very, very expensive piano. Truly expensive, but the music it produces is absolutely worth the price.
Engineering Drawings
A detailed set of engineering drawings related to urban construction.
Tracker Swarmer Specimen
A tracker swarmer specimen, lifelike and suitable for study and display.
Honeydew Melon
A large green honeydew melon, weighing about fifteen jin.
Lazy Fish
A lazy-looking fish that carries a paintbrush with it.
"Tynan's Heart"
A diamond the size and shape of a heart, shining brilliantly. An unknown excavator named it Tynan's Heart. Legend says owning it will keep your rimworld free from red errors forever.
"Randy's Tear"
A strange pearl said to have formed in the eye of someone fanatically devoted to randomness. Without question, it is priceless.
Golden Cube
A cube made of pure gold. It draws attention as if by magic, but it is said to be a replica, far removed from the effect of the original.
Golden Toilet
A golden toilet made from gold and marble. Sitting on it will supposedly drain psychic power until the user dries up into ash. That is, of course, false.
LanguageData.RSMF_CollectibleExchange_GeneralBrashDeb: General Brash's Debt Bond
General Brash's Debt Bond
Frontline Military Supply Bond
Redeem a glorious military supply bond with silver and load the weight of freedom into weapon racks and armor lockers.
Frontline Military Supply Bond
Quartermaster confirmation: this edition's bond list has been fully authorized. Equipment awaits qualified colonist receipt.
Citizen, stand straight. General Brash's bond counter is open, and today's shopping behavior will be entered into the honor file.
Welcome to the frontline supply bond window. Every piece of silver votes, and every piece of equipment speaks.
You are not here to buy things. You are here to deliver managed democracy to the faces of targets who refuse to queue.
Quartermaster notice: hesitation is not budget saving. Hesitation only lets the enemy celebrate early.
General Brash welcomes all lawful, paying citizens willing to point the muzzle in the correct direction.
A frontline bond is not a coupon. It is a handshake agreement between you and managed democracy, and it fires.
Please straighten your back, open your wallet, and leave doubt to those who have not received supplies.
What you buy today is not arms. It is a more persuasive diplomatic vocabulary.
The quartermaster has confirmed your colony still exists, so you remain qualified to keep purchasing.
Welcome back, citizen. If the last thing we sold you is still smoking, the usage direction was mostly correct.
This is not an ordinary shelf. It is a frontline solution compressed into item cards.
See the limits? That is not hunger marketing. That is the logistics officer still believing in fair distribution.
Buying weapons will not automatically bring victory, but it will make failure look very short on excuses.
The purpose of armor is not to make you invincible, but to give you enough time to finish the next slogan.
Choose a piece of equipment, then leave the remaining questions to ballistics.
If you do not know what to buy, your enemies are not working hard enough. If you want everything, you are learning quickly.
This equipment has been strictly approved by the quartermaster. At least three officials thought it looked reliable.
Please note the purchase limits. Managed democracy encourages generosity, but also encourages others to queue.
Do not ask whether it is expensive. Ask whether your colony wants to run naked into the next raid.
Every item on the weapon rack is waiting for a sufficiently loud reason.
Armor is not a symbol of fear. It is the budget result that lets courage last a few more seconds.
Firepower, armor, grenades. Together, they form a properly formatted democracy application.
Do not be frightened by the price. The truly expensive thing is letting the enemy leave your range alive.
The supply window is standing by. Your colony may stay peaceful for five more minutes, but the budget should not be idle.
If you hear silence, that is the inventory waiting for your decision with discipline.
Bond inventory will be reissued from time to time. The real question is whether you can live until the next supply round.
General Brash believes empty shelves are glory, and full shelves are an order.
Your dwell time has been recorded as careful consideration. The quartermaster recommends upgrading consideration to payment.
The enemy will not be moved by your silver savings, but they may be moved by your grenades.
Your colonists are waiting for thicker armor, or a report explaining why there is no armor.
The stock refresh countdown is advancing, and like every excellent citizen, it does not retreat.
General Brash reminds you: victory is not delivered automatically unless you click purchase first.
Silence is not peace. Silence just means nobody has set up the light machine gun yet.
If you are comparing prices, managed democracy has still granted you the honor of choice.
Transaction confirmed. The quartermaster has converted your silver into louder persuasion.
Issue approved. Let this equipment serve managed democracy, not warehouse dust.
Good, citizen. You just made the next battle's statistics look a little better.
Bond redeemed. When the drop pod arrives, receive it with the posture of a victor.
Procurement successful. Open the package, release the safety, and deliver freedom to the correct address.
The quartermaster likes your decisiveness. The enemy will soon be forced to understand it.
Payment accepted. Your colony now has one more heavy certificate of democracy.
Issued. If the equipment causes extra victory, consider it normal.
Good. General Brash approves calling this expense a strategic investment.
Transaction complete. Clear the receiving zone before the drop pod lands, or clear the enemies inside it.
Equipment has entered the delivery sequence. The quartermaster hopes you use it until resupply is required.
Insufficient silver. Patriotic enthusiasm is admirable, but accounting does not currently accept enthusiasm as payment.
The quartermaster refuses credit. Bring enough silver, then return to strengthen managed democracy.
Your budget has not reached equipment liberation standards. Go mine, go trade, go make the ledger braver.
Payment failed. Your wallet is expressing a very undemocratic objection.
Your silver reserves cannot support this ambition. Consider liberating a few ore veins first.
The quartermaster detects a funding shortage. Do not be sad. The enemy often lacks resources too.
The procurement request was intercepted by accounting. Launch a second offensive with more silver.
The current budget cannot pass. Managed democracy is not free, only very worthwhile.
You are only a little silver, and a little love for mining work, away from this equipment.
BR-16 "Voting Machine" Assault Rifle
A standard frontline tool suited for organizing objections into short bursts. Logistics advice: confirm you are standing on democracy's side before aiming.
LM-74 "Meeting Minutes" Light Machine Gun
When there are too many enemies to educate one by one, use sustained fire to hold a brief and effective field meeting.
CS-29 "Close-Range Vote" Chain Shotgun
Prepared for targets unwilling to maintain safe social distance. Every roar is a motion passed at speed.
MR-51 "Long-Range Correction" Sniper Rifle
Used at long range to correct bad positions, bad thinking, and the mistake of appearing in the center of a scope.
CR-88 "Bright Future" Charge Rifle
A high-energy beam lights the battlefield like a propaganda poster. Do not look into the muzzle unless you are checking visual loyalty on behalf of the enemy.
FG-12 "Thunderous Applause" Frag Grenade
Pull, throw, take cover. Three steps to a loud vote that even those behind cover can join.
EG-23 "Shutdown Review" EMP Grenade
A temporary silence procedure made for mechanoid targets. After throwing, enjoy their brief understanding of obedience.
CH-09 "Citizen Mind" Flak Helmet
Protects the most important battlefield asset: a head still able to follow orders. Suitable for citizens who do not plan to stop bullets with their forehead.
CV-14 "Budget Approved" Flak Vest
Covers vital organs so you may continue the reimbursement process, combat process, and victory process after being hit.
PA-66 "Bond Enforcer" Marine Armor
Heavy, expensive, and reassuring. Put it on and you are not merely a colonist. You are a mobile procurement approval result.
PH-66 "Loyalty-Sealed" Marine Helmet
Reduces shrapnel, noise, and unnecessary doubt entering the head. A transparent visor is not included. Faith does not require a wide field of view.
